title: Super Mario Odyssey
edition_pinned: Super Mario Odyssey (Nintendo Switch, 2017) — Capture + Power Moon kingdom structure as shipped at launch
moat: Capture converts enemies and objects into temporary move-sets that open moons the base kit cannot reach.

loop_skeleton.description: Traverse a kingdom with Mario's base moves, throw Cappy to capture a target, use the capture's move-set to reach a Power Moon, bank moons to fuel the Odyssey to the next kingdom when the exit threshold is met.

depth_mechanisms.name: capture move-set swap
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Throwing Cappy onto a legal target replaces Mario's actions with that target's kit (swim, fly, roll, possess object) for a limited period; moons are authored around capture tools as first-class solutions, with many also solvable by advanced base movement.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If capture is removed and only Mario's base kit remains, completion rate for moons tagged as capture-required collapses and second-session kingdom clear time rises sharply.

depth_mechanisms.name: over-supplied moon threshold
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Each kingdom offers more Power Moons than the Odyssey exit cost, so players choose which moons to bank rather than following a single mandatory path.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If moon count equals the exact exit requirement with no surplus, second-session exploration of optional moons drops because every moon becomes a choke, not a menu of options.

depth_mechanisms.name: capture-as-exploration bait
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Kingdoms seed large capturable objects and creatures that advertise new experiences (e.g. dinosaur, vehicle-like objects) so exploration seeks new capture toys, not only coin-shaped collectibles.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If capturable spectacle targets are removed and only coin/platform moons remain, average distinct capture types per session falls and retention matches a pure collectathon without move-set novelty.

feel_signature: A throw commits Cappy for a window; success is a new body with different jump, attack, or traversal rules, then a moon bank that advances the ship meter.
second_session_answer: Players return to farm surplus moons with newly unlocked captures and post-game moon types, not to re-clear a linear boss gauntlet.
packaging_lessons: Ship one kingdom with base kit plus a small capture roster before stacking multiplayer or post-game moon variants.
Set exit moon costs below available moon supply so the first clear teaches choice, not completionism.
anti_lessons: Do not treat Capture as a skin swap — if the captured form does not change legal routes or attacks, it is costume, not depth.
Do not copy moon spam without multi-solution placement; quantity without alternate solves is checklist padding.

system_bom.note: jump/throw/capture/moon bank is the core cycle per kingdom
Power Moon totals unlock Odyssey travel and later kingdoms
capture roster expands legal move-sets without a traditional skill tree
moon list and kingdom map must surface uncollected moons without forcing order
moons, captures unlocked, and kingdom state persist across sessions
fall/damage returns Mario with partial coin loss; moons already banked stay banked

citations.claim: moat/capture: Capture is the ability to control enemies, creatures, and objects via Cappy to gain new abilities and reach Power Moons.
depth_mechanisms.over-supplied moon threshold: progression banks Power Moons; more moons exist than needed to leave each kingdom.
depth_mechanisms.capture-as-exploration bait: devs describe shifting from pure star search to searching for new captures and experiences.
feel_signature / packaging: director Motokura on Power Moons hidden everywhere and capture limits.
